Title: Cellulose factories: advancing bioenergy production from forest trees

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes cellulose microfibril biosynthesis and determines cellulose deposition in primary and secondary cell walls.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA proteins form the cellulose synthase complex at the plasma membrane. 2. They use UDP-glucose to synthesize glucan chains that coalesce into cellulose microfibrils. 3. Cellulose deposition is coordinated with xylan deposition and broader cell wall formation processes.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary and secondary cell walls; wood/xylem fiber cell walls
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides structural biomass and cellulose-rich feedstock for bioenergy and biopolymer production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary based on prior functional genetic studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; gene discussed as central cellulose biosynthetic machinery across model plants and trees, not newly experimentally validated in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority target for engineering cellulose-rich biomass, but must be considered together with xylan/lignin coordination and whole-pathway regulation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports cellulose biosynthesis/deposition by editing elongating cellulose chains during synthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KORRIGAN is positioned at the cell wall side of cellulose synthesis. 2. It likely edits elongating cellulose chains as they are synthesized. 3. This function influences cellulose deposition and cell wall properties.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell wall; wood cell walls
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural biomass relevant to wood properties and bioenergy feedstock.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary based on cited functional studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a non-TF, non-core metabolic enzyme accessory factor affecting cellulose assembly.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Affects cellulose deposition and organization by interacting with cellulose during deposition.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COBRA/COBL proteins are proposed to interact with cellulose as it is deposited. 2. They may influence cellulose assembly/orientation and relay signals regulating synthesis. 3. They are part of the broader cellulose deposition machinery.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer important for biomass quality.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary based on cited functional studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanism described as thought/proposed rather than fully resolved.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Links cellulose synthase complexes with cortical microtubules, thereby influencing cellulose deposition pattern.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSI1 is a CESA-interactive protein. 2. It associates cellulose synthase complexes with cortical microtubules. 3. Through microtubule guidance it influences cellulose deposition.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural biomass for wood formation and bioenergy.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary based on cited interaction and functional studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a trafficking/cytoskeletal accessory factor, not a biosynthetic enzyme or TF.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Populus spp.; Nicotiana tabacum; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Populus spp.; Nicotiana tabacum; Populus spp./Eucalyptus spp.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased SUSY elevates UDP-glucose supply and can increase cellulose production, fiber cell wall thickness, crystallinity, biomass, or growth depending on species and source–sink context.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SUSY provides UDP-glucose substrate for cellulose synthase at the plasma membrane. 2. Upregulation in poplar increased fiber cell wall thickness and produced more cellulose with enhanced crystallinity. 3. Combined overexpression with UGP in tobacco synergistically increased plant height and biomass. 4. Phenotypic outcomes depend on source–sink sugar status, species, and physiological conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; metabolite flux interpretation; cell wall phenotyping
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Effects are context dependent and vary with source–sink relationships and species; engineering outcomes may not transfer directly between herbaceous models and trees.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In Arabidopsis, invertase rather than direct UDP-glucose production through SUSY is sufficient for normal growth; review emphasizes species-specific differences.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance cellulose-rich biomass, especially when combined with UGP or integrated with carbon partitioning strategies, but requires species-specific validation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in primer synthesis required for xylan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PARVUS is assigned to primer synthesis in Golgi-based xylan biosynthesis. 2. It contributes to production of secondary cell wall xylan coordinated with cellulose deposition.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for synthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic reaction not detailed in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter xylan structure/composition in wood biomass.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for xylan backbone elongation and thus xylan deposition in secondary cell walls.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX9 is listed among Golgi-localized proteins for xylan chain elongation. 2. Xylan biosynthesis is tightly coordinated with cellulose deposition in secondary walls.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact reaction step not expanded in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX10 is identified as a chain elongation factor in xylan biosynthesis. 2. It contributes to secondary wall carbohydrate deposition coordinated with cellulose.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for xylan chain elongation in secondary wall biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX14 is assigned to xylan chain elongation. 2. It contributes to heteroxylan biosynthesis in the Golgi.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX7 is listed as involved in xylan side-chain modification. 2. Side-chain modification helps generate heteroxylan structure in secondary walls.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX8 is included among proteins responsible for xylan side-chain modifications. 2. It affects heteroxylan structure in secondary walls.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to xylan side-chain modification during heteroxylan formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGSIP1 is listed among xylan side-chain modification proteins. 2. It contributes to heteroxylan structure in Golgi-mediated biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: sucrose to UDP-glucose supply for cellulose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases UDP-glucose supply for cellulose biosynthesis; combined overexpression with SUSY increased plant height and biomass.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGP converts glucose-1-phosphate to UDP-glucose. 2. UDP-glucose directly feeds cellulose biosynthesis by CESA. 3. Combined SUSY and UGP overexpression in tobacco produced a synergistic increase in height and biomass.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Substitutes the phosphate group of glucose-1-phosphate with UDP to form UDP-glucose.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotypic effect depends on carbon source–sink context and species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for boosting UDP-glucose supply, especially in combination with SUSY, for biomass enhancement.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts UDP-glucose to UDP-D-glucuronate, supplying precursor for UDP-xylose and xylan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGHD channels UDP-glucose toward xylan synthesis via UDP-D-glucuronate. 2. This step contributes precursor supply for heteroxylan backbone formation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces UDP-xylose, the direct backbone precursor for xylan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UXS converts UDP-D-glucuronate to UDP-xylose by decarboxylation. 2. UDP-xylose is then used as backbone substrate for xylan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes CO2 from UDP-D-glucuronate to generate UDP-xylose.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: sucrose cleavage and precursor supply for cellulose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides an indirect route from sucrose to UDP-glucose and is necessary and sufficient for normal growth in Arabidopsis, highlighting species-specific carbon partitioning into cell wall biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Invertase cleaves sucrose into glucose and fructose. 2. Glucose can be converted to UDP-glucose through downstream enzymes, supplying cellulose synthesis. 3. In Arabidopsis, invertase is necessary and sufficient for normal growth, unlike direct SUSY-derived UDP-glucose.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes sucrose to glucose and fructose.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of comparative functional studies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Emphasized as contrasting with SUSY-dependent outcomes in trees.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically regulates secondary cell wall CesA genes, promoting cellulose biosynthesis in secondary walls.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SND2 directly regulates secondary cell wall CesA genes. 2. It appears specific for secondary wall CesA genes rather than xylan or lignin genes. 3. It forms part of the transcriptional network governing xylogenesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates secondary cell wall CesA genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Specific regulator of cellulose-related secondary wall CesA expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited transcriptional studies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented in Arabidopsis framework, with broader relevance to woody plants.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically regulates secondary cell wall CesA genes, supporting cellulose deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SND3 directly regulates secondary wall CesA genes. 2. It appears specific to CesA regulation rather than xylan or lignin pathways. 3. It is part of a larger xylogenesis transcriptional network.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates secondary cell wall CesA genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Specific activator of cellulose-associated CesA transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited transcriptional studies

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically regulates secondary cell wall CesA genes and thereby cellulose biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB103 directly regulates secondary wall CesA genes. 2. It appears not to regulate xylan or lignin genes in the cited Arabidopsis studies. 3. It contributes to the secondary wall transcriptional program.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates secondary cell wall CesA genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Specific regulator of cellulose-biosynthetic CesA genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited transcriptional studies

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates in Rop/Rac GTPase-mediated cell wall feedback signaling that influences cellulose synthesis/deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RIC proteins regulate Rop/Rac GTPases in cell wall signaling. 2. This signaling network has an important role in regulation of cell wall properties including cellulose deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: feedback regulation of cell wall synthesis/deposition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of cellulose deposition through wall signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited signaling studies

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates Rop/Rac GTPase signaling involved in cell wall feedback affecting cellulose deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ROPGEF regulates Rop/Rac GTPases in cell wall signaling. 2. This network is implicated in regulation of cell wall properties relevant to cellulose synthesis/deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: feedback regulation of cell wall synthesis/deposition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream signaling activator influencing cellulose deposition indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited signaling studies

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Affects cell wall properties through wall-associated feedback signaling impacting cellulose deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THESEUS is an LRR receptor kinase involved in cell wall signaling. 2. It has been shown to impact cell wall properties. 3. It is part of feedback pathways mediating extracellular-to-cytoplasmic/nuclear signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of cell wall property responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Impacts cell wall properties through signaling/regulatory mechanisms linked to cellulose deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KOBITO/ELD1 is highlighted among LRR receptor kinase-related wall regulators. 2. It has been shown to affect cell wall properties. 3. It participates in pathways mediating feedback between the wall and intracellular compartments.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important regulator of cell wall properties with implications for cellulose deposition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary from cited functional studies
